_doc: Two-character conversation rewrite of Marketing 101 Lesson 1. SAL = veteran, booked solid (Torque Marshal voice IL22Ke355hck2I2lwmNi). MIKE = younger, great tradesman but broke (Charlie voice IKne3meq5aSn9XLyUdCD). Each beat = one animated scene (Recraft locked explainer style, style_id in recraft_style_explainer.json) animated via fal i2v (hero=Kling, else Wan). 'lines' play in order in their voice; clip covers the beat audio. 'caption' speaker-colored kinetic.

beats.scene: Two cartoon contractors standing by their work trucks in a parking lot, a frustrated younger contractor in a red shirt and a calm older veteran contractor in a blue shirt and hard hat, friendly explainer style
beats.motion: The younger contractor throws up his hands in frustration while talking, the older contractor stands calm and listens, natural body movement, fixed camera

lines.speaker: MIKE
lines.text: Sal, I don't get it. I'm a better plumber than half this town, and I'm still scraping by. You're booked solid for months. What's the trick?

beats.scene: A calm older veteran cartoon contractor in a hard hat putting a reassuring hand on a younger contractor's shoulder, parking lot, explainer style
beats.motion: The older contractor pats the younger one on the shoulder and talks warmly, slight head movement, fixed camera

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: Ain't no trick, kid. You're just missin' the one thing nobody ever taught you. Marketing.

beats.scene: A skeptical younger cartoon contractor in a red shirt crossing his arms and rolling his eyes, simple flat background, explainer style
beats.motion: The contractor crosses his arms and shakes his head skeptically while talking, fixed camera

lines.speaker: MIKE
lines.text: Marketing? Come on, Sal. I'm not buyin' billboards and dancin' on TikTok.

beats.scene: An older veteran cartoon contractor confidently explaining with open hand gestures, friendly, simple flat background, explainer style
beats.motion: The contractor gestures with both hands while explaining confidently, leaning in slightly, talking, fixed camera

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: Good. 'Cause that ain't it. Marketing's just bein' easy to find, and easy to trust, the second somebody needs you. That's the whole game.

beats.scene: A younger cartoon contractor scratching his head and thinking, a thought bubble, simple flat background, explainer style
beats.motion: The contractor scratches his head and looks up thinking, then nods slowly, fixed camera

lines.speaker: MIKE
lines.text: Findable and trustable. Okay. But how does that actually get me paid?

beats.scene: A big colorful flat funnel diagram with four stages, two cartoon contractors standing beside it pointing, explainer infographic style
beats.motion: The veteran contractor points up at a glowing funnel diagram as cartoon people-icons slide down through it, the younger contractor watches, fixed camera

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: Every customer walks the same path. We call it the funnel. A stranger becomes a visitor. A visitor raises their hand, now they're a lead. And a lead says yes. That's a booked job.

beats.scene: A younger cartoon contractor's eyes lighting up with realization, a small lightbulb above his head, explainer style
beats.motion: The contractor's eyes widen and a lightbulb pops on above his head, he points up excitedly, fixed camera

lines.speaker: MIKE
lines.text: So marketing is just gettin' more people down that path?

beats.scene: Two cartoon homeowners chatting and smiling over a backyard fence, a friendly neighborhood, explainer style
beats.motion: Two neighbors chat and gesture over a fence, both smiling and talking, leaves rustling, fixed camera

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: And here's the kicker. You already do it. Every time a happy customer tells their neighbor you didn't rip 'em off? That's word of mouth. Oldest marketing there is.

beats.scene: A younger cartoon contractor shrugging with palms up, looking unsure, simple flat background, explainer style
beats.motion: The contractor shrugs with both palms up and tilts his head while talking, fixed camera

lines.speaker: MIKE
lines.text: Yeah, but I can't control word of mouth.

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: Exactly. So we add more doors. Lemme paint you a picture.

beats.scene: A panicked cartoon homeowner in pajamas standing in a flooding garage at dawn holding a phone, water everywhere, explainer style
beats.motion: The homeowner panics, water rising around his feet, he frantically grabs his phone and taps it, fixed camera

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: Seven AM. Some poor guy's water heater just blew. Water all over the garage. What's he do?

lines.speaker: MIKE
lines.text: Grabs his phone. Types 'plumber near me.'

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: Bingo. And in ten seconds, he picks whoever shows up, has the reviews, and answers the phone. You win that moment, or you're invisible, and I book it.

lines.speaker: MIKE
lines.text: ...And you've been bookin' it this whole time.

lines.speaker: SAL
lines.text: So here's your homework. Tonight, write down your last five jobs, and where each one came from. That little list? That's your funnel, on paper. Now buy me a coffee. Lesson two's in the mornin'.
